key_insight: DeFAb turns defeasible abduction into a benchmark where symbolic verification can measure whether foundation models can revise theories correctly under defaults and exceptions.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark provides a fast, exact verifier and scalable synthetic generation pipeline that can support evaluation and training of reasoning systems beyond surface-level language performance.
method: Builds instances from 18 public knowledge sources, combining taxonomic hierarchies and behavioral property graphs.
Creates three benchmark levels with polynomial-time checks for derivation, conservativity, and minimality.
Releases harder variants plus a verifier that can also serve as an exact reward signal for DPO/RLVR/GRPO.
evidence_signal: Across 372,648+ instances, the symbolic solver achieves 100% accuracy in under 50 microseconds, while frontier models reach only 23.5% in the worst rendering-robust case and show large chain-of-thought variance.
watch_item: Because performance is sensitive to surface rendering and contamination controls, results should be interpreted carefully when comparing model generalization versus benchmark memorization.
paper_title: DeFAb: A Verifiable Benchmark for Defeasible Abduction in Foundation Models
